channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court Dismisses Sowore's Document Submission in Tinubu Defamation Case
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja rejected an attempt by Omoyele Sowore, a publisher and AAC presidential candidate, to submit uncertified documents in his defamation trial against President Tinubu. Jus...

item.title: Cyril Ramaphosa awaits court ruling on Phala Phala inquiry interdict
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa faces a critical legal juncture as the Western Cape High Court prepares to rule on his urgent application for an interdict to halt the parliamentary impeachment inquiry into ...

item.title: Success of Local Content Policy Boosts Tanzanian Involvement in Mining Sector
item.description: Minister Anthony Mavunde highlighted the success of Tanzania's local content policy, which has increased Tanzanian participation in the mining sector. He noted that over six million Tanzanians now eng...

item.title: Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial Advances as Prosecution Concludes Case
item.description: The Senate impeachment court concluded its proceedings on Article 4 against Vice President Sara Duterte on July 22, 2026, after the prosecution presented its case alleging culpable violation of the Co...

item.title: Rosie O’Donnell Shares Struggles with Trump’s Attacks and Mental Health Impact
item.description: Comedian Rosie O’Donnell has opened up about the long-term psychological effects of her 20-year feud with former President Donald Trump, describing her experience as akin to post-traumatic stress diso...

item.title: Premiers Urge Ottawa to Restore 50% Federal Funding for Healthcare
item.description: Canadian premiers from Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, and Manitoba have called on the federal government to reinstate the 50/50 funding split for healthcare, a model that was in place until the ...

item.title: Fan Culture and the Rise of Barrier Culture at Concerts
item.description: The article explores the growing phenomenon of 'barrier culture' at concerts, where fans go to extreme lengths to secure front-row seats. This includes the use of diapers to manage bodily functions, a...

item.title: Barrie Man Charged with Alleged Indecent Act
item.description: A man from Barrie, Ontario, has been charged following allegations of committing an indecent act. The incident, which occurred in the Barrie area, has prompted local authorities to investigate further...

item.title: Senator Raffy Tulfo Uses Buzzer to Regulate Speaking Time During Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial
item.description: During the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, Senator-judge Raffy Tulfo criticized the unequal allocation of speaking time among senators. He noted that opposing senators were allowed t...

item.title: Republican Rep. Andy Biggs Wins Arizona Governor Primary
item.description: Rep. Andy Biggs, a Trump-endorsed Republican, has secured the GOP nomination for Arizona governor after defeating fellow Representative David Schweikert in the primary. This victory positions Biggs to...

item.title: Arizona House Primary Results Show Lamb's Victory
item.description: In a closely contested Arizona House primary election, Republican candidate Debbie Lamb secured a decisive victory over her Democratic opponent, Mark Thompson. The race, held on July 21, 2026, saw Lam...

item.title: Linda Mwananchi Raises KSh 2.2m in 36 Hours for 2027 Presidential Bid
item.description: Linda Mwananchi, a political movement backing Edwin Sifuna for Kenya's 2027 presidential race, has raised over KSh 2.2 million in just 36 hours through a public fundraising campaign. The funds, collec...

item.title: Study Links Excessive TV Viewing in Middle Age to Brain Shrinkage and Dementia Risk
item.description: A study conducted by researchers at the University of Southern California warns that excessive television watching among middle-aged adults may lead to significant brain shrinkage, increasing the risk...

item.title: Humour and memes emerge alongside nationwide youth protests over alleged exam paper leaks and police crackdown
item.description: As protests against the alleged leaks of national competitive examination papers continued across India, demonstrators and social media users combined serious political dissent with humour and satire....

item.title: DA Announces Election Manifesto Launch in Johannesburg on August 8
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has confirmed it will unveil its election manifesto in Johannesburg on August 8, ahead of the local government elections. National campaign manager Ashor Sarupen emphasize...

item.title: Government Adjusts Fuel Prices Amid Global Market Fluctuations
item.description: The government has raised petrol prices by Rs6.39 and diesel prices by Rs7.83 per litre to reflect global market volatility and regional tensions in the Persian Gulf. The new rates, effective July 23,...

item.title: Malcolm-Jamal Warner's Widow Files Lawsuit Against His Mother Over Prenuptial Agreement Claims
item.description: Malcolm-Jamal Warner’s widow, Tenisha Warner, has filed a lawsuit against the actor’s mother, Pamela Warner, seeking to recover $1.2 million from his estate. The lawsuit, filed in DeKalb County Superi...

item.title: Dusseldorf Airport's Food Options Set New Standard for Airports
item.description: Justine Costigan explores Dusseldorf Airport (DUS), Germany's fourth-largest airport, highlighting its modern Terminal C and standout food offerings. While the airport’s efficiency and amenities are c...

item.title: South Sikkim tunnel blast kills 13, rescue efforts hindered by toxic gases and mud
item.description: A methane explosion in an under-construction tunnel of the Teesta Stage-VI Hydel Project in South Sikkim, India, has resulted in 13 fatalities with 12 workers still missing. Rescue teams faced extreme...

item.title: Anna Hazare urges minister's resignation amid CJP protests and NEET exam controversies
item.description: Social activist Anna Hazare has written to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, urging the government to engage in dialogue and consider the resignation of ministers as a measure for accountability. The lett...
